name: harness-results-output
description: finalize_test_results() in lib/common.sh copies LAST_OUTPUT to RESULTS_DIR/<test>/raw.jsonl, writes summary.json (test_name/passed/failed/assertions), writes transcript.md extracting text/tool_use blocks; init_test_results() creates the dir. run-harness.sh exports RESULTS_DIR=harness/results/, writes harness-report.json after summary.

name: harness-run-claude-flags
description: run_claude() flags in order: --plugin-dir, --output-format stream-json, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --no-session-persistence, --model sonnet, --max-budget-usd 0.50, --max-turns 3. scaffold_reset() also removes PRISM_TEST_DIR/.prism to ensure clean brain state between tests.

content: prism-harness Python package structure: prism_harness/ with cli.py (argparse subcommands: run/parse/report/list), claude_session.py (run_claude wrapping subprocess), assertions.py (AssertionContext tracking pass/fail/skip), parser.py (parse_jsonl, extract_tool_calls/text/hooks/turns, deep_get), reporter.py (render_transcript, write_summary, finalize_results, harness-report.json), scaffold.py (Scaffold class brownfield/greenfield), tests/ (one module per test, each exposes NAME and run(ctx, scaffold, plugin_dir, results_dir)). Results saved to timestamped dirs with last symlink.
prism-harness --dry-run flag: _cmd_run() in cli.py checks dry_run before pre-flight checks; lists matching test modules by NAME and prints TC lines from docstrings without invoking claude.

name: harness-claude-session-env-fix
description: claude_session.py run_claude(): strip both CLAUDECODE and CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT from env to prevent nested session errors. Use stderr=subprocess.STDOUT to capture all stream-json output (which may route through stderr when stdout is redirected to file).

name: harness-verbose-flag-required
description: claude_session.py run_claude() cmd requires --verbose when using --output-format stream-json with -p; without it claude errors: 'When using --print, --output-format=stream-json requires --verbose'

name: harness-model-default-subscription
description: claude_session.py run_claude() model/max_budget_usd params: defaults are empty string/0.0 — only appended to cmd when explicitly set, so harness uses Claude Code subscription model by default without needing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

name: assert-init-skills-lacks-pattern
description: assert_init_skills_lacks: checks init event skills+slash_commands arrays (not raw output) to avoid false SKIPs from incidental tool-result directory listings. Falls through to assistant text check when skill is system-registered but may be PRISM-filtered. Pattern: absent from both arrays OR absent from assistant text → PASS; found in assistant text → SKIP.

name: prism-loop-max-turns-5
description: prism-loop TC-3 harness invocation needs max_turns=5 (not the default 3) to give the loop enough turns to create the state file (.claude/prism-loop.local.md). max_turns=3 was too low for prism-loop init to complete.

name: parser-subagent-extractors
description: parser.py sub-agent extractor pattern: extract_subagent_spawns() finds Agent name=='Agent' tool_use blocks; extract_hook_events() handles both structured (subtype='hook_progress') and text-embedded ('hook_progress: name: body') system events; extract_sfr_certificates() regex-matches SFR section headers case-insensitively with optional markdown markers; correlate_subagent_lifecycle() matches spawn↔start↔stop by tool_use_id with positional fallback

content: test-brain-preload uses extract_hook_messages() to detect brain_context in system events; falls back to raw content scan; skips gracefully if Brain not bootstrapped
test-gate-validation uses max_turns=8 to reach gate; skips gracefully if prism-loop doesn't reach gate step; checks both assistant text and raw JSONL for PASS/FAIL

brain-preload.jsonl and gate-validation.jsonl fixtures validated via prism-harness validate: 36/36 PASS; fixture format: system event with brain_context or gate keywords, Bash tool_use, assistant response with decision text

test_customer_skill_lifecycle.py pattern: use extended phase sets (VERIFY_SKILL_NAMES | {'verify','test'}) for AC assertions because top-level phase skills compete for the 5-skill cap with sub-skills; cold-start keyword matching brings in top-level skills (verify, test, build) before sub-skills appear in fixture order

customer-skills.jsonl SHIP sub-skills updated from (package, deploy, release) to (ci, docs, handoff) to match real TalentSyncPro index per prism-2c02 spec; ci/docs/handoff have zero keyword coverage in _STEP_SKILL_KEYWORDS — documented as known coverage gaps in AC-19 tests

test_multi_turn_lifecycle.py pattern: _make_conductor_with_step_scores(step_scores) uses MagicMock side_effect with fallback to merged globals; tests requiring engine per-step support marked xfail(strict=False) with _conductor_has_score_threshold() / _conductor_passes_step_id_to_brain() runtime guards

conftest.py learning trajectory: _print_learning_trajectory() prints separate section only when test_multi_turn_lifecycle tests ran; shows cold vs learned skill name diffs per step using merged FEATURE_A_USAGE scores (delta column is 0 until score_threshold lands)
